config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society is important, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary by default, because that opens the door to abuse by governments or majorities. In my view, a healthy society protects basic rights first and then asks people to contribute to the common good. Social interests matter, but they should not override fundamental individual freedoms except in limited, clearly justified cases.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Bagi saya di Amerika Serikat, hak individu harus menjadi titik awal, bukan sesuatu yang mudah dikorbankan demi “kepentingan masyarakat.” Sejarah politik kami penuh dengan contoh bahwa ketika mayoritas atau pemerintah diberi terlalu banyak kuasa atas nama kebaikan bersama, hasilnya sering merugikan kelompok kecil dan membatasi kebebasan dasar. Itu tidak berarti masyarakat tidak penting; berarti kepentingan publik harus dibuktikan benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan tidak melanggar hak inti. Jadi saya menolak pernyataan itu jika dimaknai sebagai prioritas umum atas individu, karena menurut saya masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dengan melindungi individu terlebih dahulu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in Indonesia we have seen how appeals to “the common good” can be used to silence dissent or pressure minorities. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights exist in a vacuum: rights should come with responsibility, especially when one person’s freedom harms others or the public order. For me, the fair position is that society can limit individual rights only in narrow, clearly justified cases, not as a default rule. If we reverse that balance, it becomes too easy for the state or the majority to decide whose rights matter less.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu jika dijadikan prinsip umum, karena bagi saya di Amerika Serikat hak individu harus tetap menjadi dasar, bukan dikalahkan oleh klaim “kepentingan masyarakat” yang terlalu mudah dipakai. Saya paham argumen tentang tanggung jawab sosial dan perlunya pembatasan dalam kasus tertentu, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, jelas, dan benar-benar perlu, bukan asumsi default. Pengalaman politik di AS membuat saya sangat curiga ketika mayoritas atau pemerintah diberi terlalu banyak ruang untuk memutuskan bahwa kebebasan pribadi bisa dikesampingkan demi kebaikan bersama. Masyarakat yang sehat tidak dibangun dengan mengorbankan individu terlebih dahulu, melainkan dengan melindungi hak dasar mereka sambil menuntut tanggung jawab yang proporsional.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my perspective in Indonesia, I have seen how “public interest” can be used to pressure minorities, silence criticism, or excuse unfair treatment, so I do not trust that phrase when it becomes a blank check for the state or the majority. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every situation, because one person’s freedom should not directly harm others or public order. So I would put basic rights first, and allow limits only when they are narrow, necessary, and clearly justified.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Amerika Serikat saya belajar bahwa hak individu harus menjadi dasar, bukan sesuatu yang mudah dikalahkan atas nama kepentingan masyarakat. Argumen “demi kebaikan bersama” sering terdengar masuk akal, tetapi dalam praktiknya bisa dipakai pemerintah atau mayoritas untuk membatasi kebebasan, terutama bagi kelompok kecil yang tidak populer. Saya tidak bilang hak individu absolut tanpa batas; kalau ada bahaya nyata bagi orang lain atau ketertiban umum, pembatasan bisa dibenarkan, tetapi harus sempit, perlu, dan proporsional. Jadi bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menempatkan individu di bawah kolektif, melainkan yang melindungi hak dasar dulu lalu menuntut tanggung jawab sosial.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
